sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Atlético Madrid are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with notable wins against Real Madrid (5-2) and Eintracht Frankfurt (5-1). Osasuna, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing only 1 win in their last 5 matches, with losses to Real Sociedad (2-0) and Real Betis (2-0). This gives Atlético a clear edge heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been split, with Osasuna winning 2-0 at home in May 2025 and Atlético securing a narrow 1-0 victory in January 2025. Historically, Atlético have dominated with 3 wins in the last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting they often find a way to edge out Osasuna.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atlético Madrid rank 4th in the league for goals scored (15) and boast high possession (54.4%) and chance creation (26 big chances created). Osasuna, positioned 12th, have scored just 7 goals this season and struggle defensively, conceding in their last 5 matches. Atlético's superior attacking and defensive stats further tilt the balance in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Julián Álvarez has been in scintillating form for Atlético, scoring 6 goals and providing 2 assists in the last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 8.14. For Osasuna, Iker Benito offers some threat with a 7.8 rating, but their lack of consistent firepower could be a concern against Atlético’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid's strong home form, unbeaten streak, and superior season stats, they are clear favorites with Sofascore predicting a 65% chance of a home win. Osasuna's recent struggles and inability to keep clean sheets suggest they will find it tough at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano. Expect a competitive game, but Atlético should have the upper hand.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, the score remains level at 0-0 between Atlético Madrid and Osasuna at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano. Despite the lack of goals, the first half showcased a clear disparity in control, with the home side dominating possession and creating more opportunities. Atlético Madrid have been the more aggressive team, while Osasuna have struggled to impose themselves consistently, focusing primarily on defensive organization. Both teams have yet to convert their chances into goals, leaving everything to play for in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Atlético Madrid's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid have been the dominant force in the opening 45 minutes, controlling the majority of the ball and pushing forward with intent. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area indicate a strong attacking presence, though they've missed a key opportunity to take the lead. Players like Antoine Griezmann and Julián Alvarez have been pivotal in testing Osasuna’s defense, with several efforts on target. However, their finishing has lacked the necessary precision, and they'll need to be more clinical in the second half to break the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna have adopted a more conservative approach in the first half, focusing on defensive solidity over attacking ambition. They've managed to limit Atlético Madrid's clear-cut chances despite being under pressure for significant periods. Their forays into the final third have been far less frequent, and they've struggled to create meaningful opportunities, with only a solitary shot on target. While their defensive structure has held firm so far, they’ll need to offer more going forward if they hope to steal a result in Madrid.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in favor of Atlético Madrid for the majority of the time, with sustained periods of pressure in the early stages and towards the latter part of the half. They displayed consistent attacking intent with several spells of dominance, particularly in the middle and closing minutes. Osasuna had their moments, especially in brief bursts around the midway point, where they managed to push back and relieve some pressure. However, their attacking spells were short-lived, and they often reverted to a defensive posture under Atlético's relentless pursuit.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Antoine Griezmann stands out as a key figure in their attacking setup, having already tested the Osasuna defense with dangerous efforts. Julián Alvarez has also been heavily involved and could be a decisive factor if given the right opportunity. On the Osasuna side, goalkeeper Sergio Herrera has been instrumental in keeping the scoreline level with a standout performance. Up front, Ante Budimir will need to be more involved if Osasuna are to pose a threat in the second half, as his physical presence could trouble Atlético's backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Atlético Madrid are likely to continue their aggressive approach, leveraging their home advantage and superior possession to push for a breakthrough. If they can sharpen their finishing, they have a strong chance of finding the net. Osasuna, on the other hand, may look to exploit counter-attacking opportunities, especially if Atlético overcommit in attack. Their ability to maintain defensive discipline while capitalizing on rare chances will be crucial. Expect the home side to maintain control, but Osasuna could surprise if they seize a moment of transition.
